title: Comanthera ciliata
taxonomy: Comanthera ciliata is classified in the family Eriocaulaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as pipeworts. This family belongs to the order Poales, which is a large and diverse group of monocotyledonous plants. The order Poales is further classified under the class Liliopsida, also known as the monocots. The class Liliopsida is a part of the larger division Magnoliophyta, which includes all flowering plants. Overall, the classification of Comanthera ciliata reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the monocot clade.
uses: Comanthera ciliata is native to Brazil, and its medicinal properties have been studied for its potential benefits. intro: Comanthera ciliata, also known as Brazilian button grass, is a species of flowering plant in the family Eriocaulaceae. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to reduce pain and alleviate symptoms of various infections. The bioactive constituents of Comanthera ciliata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Comanthera ciliata is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
